name: scan_ocr_online_version_aware_triangulation
principle: The scan image is the primary record. OCR is a transcription. Online sources are independent witnesses and may correct OCR only when identity and edition scope are explicit.

verified_exact: scan image checksum and physical PDF page are recorded
primary structured OCR and at least one independent OCR witness are recorded
document identity is matched to an official or academic source
online text is the exact document/edition or the checked string is an edition-stable fact
names, titles, dates, numerals and item order have no unresolved conflict
a human reviewer confirms high-risk quotations and every conflict resolution
publish_with_warning: the scan is legible enough for a reasoned human judgment
all conflicting OCR and online readings are retained
uncertainty note explains the disputed characters or version boundary
citation_allowed remains false for verbatim quotation until exact verification
forbidden: silently replacing historical wording with a newer edition
using search snippets as the only text witness
using an LLM rewrite as documentary evidence
marking a whole document verified because a sample page passed
discarding a minority OCR reading before visual review

ocr_witness_thresholds.independence: Apple Vision receives only a freshly rendered scan image and never receives primary OCR text, corrections, or dictionaries derived from the primary result.

requirements: inspect every engine conflict against the rendered scan
record the accepted reading and rejected alternatives
reconstruct tables cell by cell rather than accepting flattened reading order
keep citation_allowed false when exact-edition online corroboration is unavailable

ocr_witness_thresholds.document_release: A sampled page never promotes a document. Every published page needs its own gate; any unresolved page remains unavailable to citation-locked AI.
ocr_witness_thresholds.blank_pages: Both OCR engines returning empty is not enough. A human must confirm the rendered page is blank or a divider.
